tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Blind Adults Read Again After Pioneering Stanford Implant Restores Their Sight
stories.summary: Twenty-six formerly blind patients joyfully recovered vision through a revolutionary tiny implant, reading and recognizing faces once more.

stories.headline: After Hurricane Helene, One Woman Recovers Lost Treasures and Reunites Heartbroken Families
stories.summary: Former raft guide Mandy Wallace now recovers treasures from flooded rivers, recently reuniting a handmade wooden sign carved with a napping cat back to the Hedricks family.

stories.headline: Community WhatsApp Group Pivots Wheelchair Fund to Send Disabled Boy to School
stories.summary: When a child received his needed wheelchair, a compassionate group redirected their fundraising to ensure his education continues uninterrupted.

stories.headline: Tigers Roar Back in Manas: Population Surges From 14 to Nearly 60 in a Decade
stories.summary: Tigers in Manas National Park surged from just 14 in 2015 to nearly 60 today, thanks to community collaboration that helped both wildlife and local people flourish together.

stories.headline: U.S. Adaptive Open Proves Golf's True Spirit: Perseverance, Joy, and Unwavering Support
stories.summary: At the U.S. Adaptive Open, 96 golfers from around the world compete with no prize money, including Max Togisala who shot a 67 from a three-wheeled golf cart.

stories.headline: Scientists Engineer Endless Supply of Cancer-Fighting Immune Cells
stories.summary: USC scientists engineered renewable cancer-fighting immune cells that can self-renew in the lab and be genetically engineered to recognize tumors, challenging decades of biological assumptions.

stories.headline: Company Donates Free Central Air to Veteran, Turning Empathy Into Cool Relief
stories.summary: When veteran Charles Hicks struggled to breathe without cooling, Tyler's Heating and Cooling—owned by a fellow veteran—donated and installed free central air conditioning.

stories.headline: Bank Employees Turn Gas Cards Into Meals for Seniors
stories.summary: Bank employees donated their gift cards to Meals On Wheels, raising $3,000 to keep seniors fed during expensive times.

stories.headline: Teen's Second-Life Mission: 5,300 Used Tennis Balls Find Purpose in Schools and Shelters
stories.summary: Seventeen-year-old Max Shilt has repurposed over 5,300 tennis balls destined for landfills, giving them new life in schools and animal shelters.

stories.headline: Surgeons Control Humanoid Robots to Perform World-First Operation on Live Subjects
stories.summary: Surgeons at UC San Diego remotely controlled humanoid robots to remove gallbladders from live pigs, offering hope for affordable surgical care in under-resourced hospitals.

stories.headline: Critically Endangered Great Indian Bustard Chick Passes Critical Survival Phase
stories.summary: A critically endangered Great Indian Bustard chick survived past its dangerous 40-day window, offering fresh hope for one of Earth's rarest birds.

stories.headline: 79 Endangered Hawksbill Sea Turtle Hatchlings Released Into the Ocean
stories.summary: Alert citizens spotted 79 hawksbill sea turtle hatchlings near Progreso's 71st street, prompting authorities to rescue and safely release them into the ocean.
